steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.to invent: While English 'invent' derives from it, 'invenire' means 'to find/discover'

hints.present indicative: Present 1st person would be 'invenio' — 'inveni' is perfect
hints.future: Future would be 'inveniam' — 'inveni' is perfect indicative

hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are principal parts of 'venio' (to come) — 'invenio' adds the prefix 'in-'
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are principal parts of 'video' (to see)
hints.voco, vocare, vocavi, vocatum: These are principal parts of 'voco' (to call)

hints.nominative: Nominative is 'fides' — 'fidem' with -em ending is accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'fide' — 'fidem' is accusative, direct object of 'inveni'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'fidei' ends in -ei
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'fidei' ends in -ei
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'fidei' ends in -ei

translation: I have not found such great faith in Israel
